name: College Tuition and Post-Graduation Outcomes: Analyzing the Financial Return on Higher Education
description: An integrated dataset combining the U.S. Department of Education College Scorecard, IPEDS Institutional Characteristics (HD2023), and IPEDS Student Financial Aid (SFA2223) to analyze the relationship between college tuition costs, student debt, and post-graduation earnings across U.S. postsecondary institutions.

isBasedOn.name: College Scorecard Institution-Level Data, Most Recent Cohorts
IPEDS Student Financial Aid and Net Price Survey 2022-23 (SFA2223)

variableMeasured.name: UNITID
variableMeasured.description: Unique identification number assigned to each postsecondary institution by the U.S. Department of Education

variableMeasured.name: STABBR
variableMeasured.description: Two-letter state abbreviation where the institution is located

variableMeasured.name: TUITIONFEE_IN
variableMeasured.description: Published in-state tuition and fees in US dollars

variableMeasured.name: TUITIONFEE_OUT
variableMeasured.description: Published out-of-state tuition and fees in US dollars

variableMeasured.name: DEBT_MDN
variableMeasured.description: Median cumulative loan debt at graduation in US dollars

variableMeasured.name: MD_EARN_WNE_P10
variableMeasured.description: Median earnings of students working and not enrolled 10 years after entry, in US dollars

variableMeasured.name: FLOAN_A
variableMeasured.description: Total dollar amount of federal loans distributed to students at the institution

measurementTechnique: Administrative data aggregation, federal survey collection
conditionsOfAccess: Open access. All source datasets are publicly released U.S. federal government data in the public domain.
